rules.name: no-hardware-touch-in-integration-edit
rules.evidence: 1. **HA integration** (`custom_components/crop_steering/`) — entities, config-flow wizard, pure calculations, service events. Never touches hardware.

rules.name: no-hardware-touch-in-integration-write
rules.evidence: 1. **HA integration** (`custom_components/crop_steering/`) — entities, config-flow wizard, pure calculations, service events. Never touches hardware.

rules.name: commit-conventional-with-coauthor
rules.evidence: **Commit style:** conventional commits (`feat:`/`fix:`/`docs:`/`chore:`) with a `Co-Authored-By: Claude` trailer when written via Claude Code.

rules.name: changelog-dual-view-edit
rules.evidence: Every release in `CHANGELOG.md` leads with **🌱 In plain English** (anyone can follow it) then **🔧 Technical notes** (entity/code detail). Keep both when adding a release.

rules.name: changelog-dual-view-write
rules.evidence: Every release in `CHANGELOG.md` leads with **🌱 In plain English** (anyone can follow it) then **🔧 Technical notes** (entity/code detail). Keep both when adding a release.

rules.name: controller-state-field-migration-safety-edit
rules.evidence: Keep it that way. Adding a state field → add it to `_fresh_zone` with a safe default and read it with `.get(k)` / `is not None`; never assume it exists in an old file.

rules.name: controller-state-field-migration-safety-write
rules.evidence: Keep it that way. Adding a state field → add it to `_fresh_zone` with a safe default and read it with `.get(k)` / `is not None`; never assume it exists in an old file.

rules.name: addon-option-backward-compat-edit
rules.evidence: Never require the operator to wipe state, re-run setup, reconfigure, or hand-migrate a schema.

rules.name: addon-option-backward-compat-write
rules.evidence: Never require the operator to wipe state, re-run setup, reconfigure, or hand-migrate a schema.

rules.name: entity-changes-additive-edit
rules.evidence: Integration entity changes → additive. Renaming/removing an entity an old install relies on is breaking; avoid or migrate.

rules.name: entity-changes-additive-write
rules.evidence: Integration entity changes → additive. Renaming/removing an entity an old install relies on is breaking; avoid or migrate.

rules.name: no-hardcoded-f2-values-edit
rules.evidence: F2-specific values are **defaults/overrides, never hardcoded assumptions**: entity ids (`switch.veg_main_pump`, `sensor.atlas_legacy_1_ec`, …)… A change that only works because of F2's exact names or numbers is a bug.

rules.name: no-hardcoded-f2-values-write
rules.evidence: F2-specific values are **defaults/overrides, never hardcoded assumptions**: entity ids (`switch.veg_main_pump`, `sensor.atlas_legacy_1_ec`, …)… A change that only works because of F2's exact names or numbers is a bug.

uncompilable: Dev commands (lint/format/test) listed without an attached before/after obligation — usage catalog, not a rule
Architecture / critical-files list — descriptive reference, no directive
Phase logic (P0–P3) — domain description, not tool-bindable
Hardware control sequence — descriptive engine behavior, not a directive to Claude
Key entity naming patterns — documentation catalog, no explicit obligation language
Deploy = Rebuild not Restart — operational Supervisor-UI action, not a repo Bash/Edit/Write call
Prove it: test-migration/version-consistency requirement — duplicate of the in-place-upgrade rule, no distinct trigger
Verify shot length from the live pump, not from 'deployed' — operational verification guidance, not a repo tool call
Shot sizing PER-PLANT block entry — HA UI configuration instruction, not a code edit
F2 facts (3 zones × 36 plants, etc.) — reference data only
Historical note on retired intelligence layer — descriptive history, no directive
General 'every change must upgrade in place and work zero-setup' — too general, no specific file/action trigger
Fresh install works with no extra layers — general design principle, no specific trigger
F2 must set feed_ec_sensor/feed_ph_sensor in add-on Configuration — Supervisor UI runtime config, not a repo file edit
